thesisFit.text: ECC targets the AI-infrastructure tooling layer: a profiler that instruments every agent hop — tool calls, model routes, token cost — with zero prompt changes [c2], auto-routing each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval [c3], and dropping in beside existing harnesses with no rewrite or lock-in [c5]. That cross-harness, harness-adjacent positioning is a clean fit for a pre-seed AI-infra thesis, and the one portability claim underpinning it is verified [c5]. Latency/cost reduction without touching prompts is the stated core promise [c1], though that headline is not yet independently supported.

citations.quote: ECC is a profiler that instruments every agent hop (tool calls, model routes, token cost) with zero prompt changes.
ECC auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.
ECC drops in beside existing harnesses with no rewrite and no lock-in (cross-harness portability).
ECC cuts agent latency and token spend without touching your prompts.

team.text: Team is a single person: Supawich is stated as the solo founder and sole author of the ECC harness [c12], a claim that is itself unsupported. The deterministic founder axis scores 82 and is trending up (61→82 since 2025-10-01), signalling a returning founder, but no bios, prior outcomes, or hires are disclosed. Cap table is explicitly undisclosed. Solo-founder concentration is the primary team risk to underwrite.

citations.quote: Supawich is the solo founder and sole author of the ECC harness.

market.text: Market is AI-infrastructure tooling. Traction cited is early and mostly unsupported: 9 design partners running ECC against their own stacks [c9], a median 38% token-cost reduction across partner workloads [c10], and 410 GitHub stars with 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks [c11] — none independently verified. Pricing is stated as per traced agent-run [c7], also unsupported. Of five market/traction claims only one is verified, and ARR is explicitly undisclosed, so revenue scale cannot be assessed.

citations.quote: 9 design partners are running ECC against their own agent stacks.
A median 38% token-cost reduction was observed across partner workloads.
ECC has 410 GitHub stars and 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks.
ECC is priced per traced agent-run.

product.text: The shipped, verifiable core is the harness instrumentation, which is stated as shipped and open-sourced [c8] and portable across harnesses with no lock-in [c5] — both verified. Around that sit unsupported product claims: the cheapest-passing-model auto-router [c3] and a regression gate that blocks any change making an agent slower or more expensive [c4]. Critically, the headline performance claim is contradicted: 'ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production' [claim-slide4-sub100ms] is contradicted by repo evidence, undercutting the named wow moment (the live latency board) and the core latency promise [c1]. Product is real at the instrumentation layer but its speed/optimization differentiators are the contradicted or unproven part.

citations.quote: ECC harness instrumentation is shipped and open-sourced.
ECC drops in beside existing harnesses with no rewrite and no lock-in (cross-harness portability).
A regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.
ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.

risks.text: Chief risk is the one contradicted claim: sub-100ms production routing [claim-slide4-sub100ms] is contradicted by repo evidence, so the central latency selling point does not hold up. Diligence risk compounds it — the token-savings result [c10], design-partner count [c9], and GitHub traction [c11] are all unsupported, and pricing [c7] and the router/gate mechanics [c3][c4] are unproven. Structural risks: solo founder [c12], undisclosed ARR, and undisclosed cap table. The Idea-vs-Market axis scores 39, dragged down by the contradiction.

citations.quote: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.
A median 38% token-cost reduction was observed across partner workloads.
9 design partners are running ECC against their own agent stacks.
ECC has 410 GitHub stars and 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks.
Supawich is the solo founder and sole author of the ECC harness.

recommendation: Verified open-source harness and a strong, improving solo founder, but the headline sub-100ms claim is contradicted and nearly all traction is unsupported — pass unless the latency contradiction is resolved and partner/token-savings data is confirmed.
